source: Android PolarUserDeviceSettings.fromBytes and iOS PolarUserDeviceSettings.fromProto characterization

expected.commonDecision: shared common parsing should preserve protobuf field presence: omitted automatic-training numeric fields and AUTOS state are nullable in the shared model. Android facade code may apply legacy defaults when exposing the current Android public API.

notes: Android currently defaults omitted training numeric fields to 0 and omitted AUTOS state to true. iOS currently defaults omitted USB connection settings and automatic-training detection state to false while keeping omitted training numeric fields and AUTOS nil when the corresponding messages are absent. Shared shared code should use nullable presence-preserving semantics and leave platform compatibility defaults in platform facades.
